mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand across consumption and investment channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day equity returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production levels above the 100 index baseline during a late-cycle expansion signal capacity constraints and impending supply bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 100 consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, contradicting the bottleneck mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum consistently predicts positive 20-day equity returns across all macro regimes (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth falling below 100,000 for two consecutive months indicates weakening labor demand and reduced household income growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 70% of months where PAYEMS growth remains below the 100k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales confirm synchronized strength in production and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 60-day equity returns (t-stat < 1.5 over 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month housing starts decline exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential construction activity and related supply chains, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts drops above 5 percent consistently precede positive 5-day equity returns during recovery phases.
